vBRIEFInfo.description: Story vBRIEF for re-enabling Deft local branch-protection policy in the directive framework repo.

narratives.Description: Re-enable the Deft local branch-policy gate for the directive framework repository so local hooks and checks no longer permit default-branch commits by project policy. This is a narrow governance change that should be handled independently from the installer and GitHub-body tooling stories.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: Update `vbrief/PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json` so `plan.policy.allowDirectCommitsToMaster` resolves to `false`. Verify the policy rendering and branch gate behavior through the existing task surfaces without changing unrelated project configuration.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want Deft local branch protection enforced, so that local commits follow the same branch-first workflow expected by the repository.

items.title: Disable direct default-branch commits in project policy

narrative.Acceptance: Given the directive project policy, when `task policy:show -- --field=plan.policy.allowDirectCommitsToMaster` runs, then it reports `current: false`.

items.title: Verify branch policy gates still run

narrative.Acceptance: Given the updated policy, when `task verify:branch` runs from a feature branch, then it passes while still resolving the typed policy field from `PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json`.

expected_outputs: branch verification passes on feature branch
swarm.missing_traces_justification: User requested re-enabling Deft branch policy during the WSL swarm-readiness session.

references.title: User request: flip local Deft branch policy back on during branch work
